name: ExtensionDevice_Type
title: R5: type
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Device.type` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Device.type` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Device.type` 0..* `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `Device.type` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4: `Device.type` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Device.type` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Device.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Device.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `Device.type` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Device.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Device.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
Multiple device types are use when a device is categorized in more than one context – for example, hybrid devices in which the device is both of type gateway and sensor.

element.short: The kind or type of device
element.definition: The kind or type of device. A device instance may have more than one type - in which case those are the types that apply to the specific instance of the device.
element.comment: Multiple device types are use when a device is categorized in more than one context – for example, hybrid devices in which the device is both of type gateway and sensor.
